_meta.purpose: The STRATEGY game-knowledge KB — sibling to frlg_gates.json. It is the data the StrategicPlanner (pokemon_planner.py) reads to turn 'what's ahead of her' into PROACTIVE prep WANTS, surfaced through the same `place`/oracle seam the folklore + questline notes use. This is the 'she read every strategy guide' layer: gym/E4/Champion rosters + counters, keeper-species quality + where to catch them, and the handful of game-changing TMs. Portable-engine split (CLAUDE.md rule 14): FireRed FACTS live HERE; the planner engine is game-agnostic and reads this file.
_meta.source: pret/pokefirered (data/trainers, party rosters) + Bulbapedia leader/E4 pages + long-standing FRLG playthrough knowledge, distilled offline. FireRed version (Vulpix/Growlithe/Scyther available; Sandshrew/Meowth/Pinsir are LeafGreen).
_meta.firewall: MODE-GATED. Consumed only inside the Pokémon campaign free-roam oracle context. Never touches core Kira identity; never surfaces in unrelated chat. Same firewall class as frlg_gates.json.
_meta.voice_rule: The planner renders these facts as ONE characterful anticipation beat in HER voice (excited-kid-who-did-her-homework), never a data dump. The `counter`/`folklore` strings are her raw material, not lines to read verbatim.
_meta.type_note: `weak_to` = attack types that hit the leader's overall team hard (a curated summary, not a per-mon chart). `answer_types` = the practical types SHE should field to beat them. `answer_species` = specific keepers that hard-counter (used for the 'level your existing counter' nudge). `level_band` = [low, high] of the leader's team on the first (non-rematch) fight.

Brock.counter: his Rock/Ground team folds to Grass or Water moves; a Bulbasaur or a caught Mankey/Nidoran walks all over him
Brock.folklore: he runs ROCK-types — folks say fire and flying moves bounce right off; grass or water is how challengers get through

Misty.counter: her Water team hates Electric and Grass — a Pikachu or a grass starter shreds Starmie
Misty.folklore: she runs WATER-types — a whole gym over a pool; grass and electric do the work

Lt. Surge.counter: Electric can't touch a GROUND-type at all — a Diglett/Dugtrio from the cave, or any ground mon, makes Surge a joke
Lt. Surge.folklore: the lightning American — ELECTRIC-types; ground moves shut his whole act down

Erika.counter: Grass/Poison melts to Fire and to Flying/Psychic — a Growlithe or a Fire move is a clean sweep
Erika.folklore: the flower lady — GRASS and poison; fire melts her garden, flyers do fine too

Koga.counter: his Poison team gets erased by PSYCHIC (and Ground); an Abra line here is the answer that carries straight into Sabrina
Koga.folklore: a literal ninja — POISON and confusion tricks; bring antidotes and patience, and Psychic wrecks him

Sabrina.counter: Psychic-types fear Bug and Ghost — a Gengar (Ghost/Poison) or a strong fast physical hitter is the play; her Alakazam is glass, out-speed or out-muscle it
Sabrina.folklore: people whisper about her — PSYCHIC-types, sees your moves coming; ghosts and bugs are the folk remedy

Blaine.counter: his Fire team washes out to WATER (or Rock/Ground); a Lapras/Water move puts out the whole volcano
Blaine.folklore: the old fire scientist — FIRE-types on a volcano island; water washes him right out

Giovanni.counter: Ground-types drown to WATER, GRASS and ICE — a Grass starter or a Water/Ice hitter takes the last badge
Giovanni.folklore: nobody talks straight about the Viridian leader — GROUND-types, they say, and something colder underneath

Lorelei.counter: the Elite Four opens with Ice-and-Water — Electric and Grass hit her water mons hard; Grass eats her Lapras, and a strong physical hitter cracks the ice
Lorelei.folklore: the Elite Four opens with an ice-and-water lady — they say lightning and grass are how you get past her

Bruno.counter: second seat is Fighting/Rock — PSYCHIC blows his fighters away; his two Onix drown to Water or Grass
Bruno.folklore: second is the muscle — Fighting and Rock; a Psychic type turns his whole gym to jelly

Agatha.counter: third is Ghost/Poison — PSYCHIC and GROUND both hit her hard; watch out, Normal moves do NOTHING to her ghosts, so bring the right tool
Agatha.folklore: third is the ghost-woman — Ghost and Poison; Psychic answers both, and don't bother swinging Normal moves at ghosts

Lance.counter: the dragon master — and ICE is the dragon-slayer: an Ice Beam / Blizzard is 4x on his Dragonite and shreds his Dragonairs; Electric handles his Gyarados and Aerodactyl. An Ice-type like Lapras is EXACTLY his counter
Lance.folklore: fourth is the dragon master — and everyone says the same thing: ice is the only thing a dragon truly fears

Gary.counter: the Champion — your rival, and his team is MIXED (Pidgeot, Alakazam, Rhydon, an Eeveelution or Arcanine/Exeggutor/Gyarados, and his starter's final form). No single answer: this is the fight your whole balanced, levelled SIX exists for. His Alakazam is glass — hit it fast; his Rhydon falls to Water/Grass; save your best for his ace
Gary.folklore: and at the very top — HIM. Gary. The rival. Whatever his team is by now, this is the one the whole journey's been building to

species_quality._doc: keeper flags for the opportunistic-catch judgment (roster_judgment). tier: rare_strong (grab it) / strong / project (weak now, monster later) / decent. `where` is the usual catch spot; `evolves` names the payoff.

pikachu.note: rare AND genuinely strong — the iconic Electric, and a clean Misty/Surge-water answer

abra.where: Routes 24/25 by Cerulean (it Teleports — catch fast)
abra.note: becomes ALAKAZAM, one of the best sweepers in the game; carries Koga, Sabrina and the E4

growlithe.note: a loyal Fire powerhouse — Arcanine is a monster and the clean Erika answer

scyther.note: a rare Bug/Flying glass cannon — fast and hits hard, a Sabrina answer

eevee.where: gift in Celadon (the house above the Game Corner)
eevee.note: one-of-a-kind — evolve it into exactly the coverage the team is missing

dratini.where: Safari Zone fishing / Game Corner prize (Dratini, coins)
dratini.note: the pseudo-legendary — a long project but ends as a DRAGONITE, a top-tier ace

lapras.where: gift from a Silph Co. employee in Saffron (Team Rocket raid)
lapras.note: a gift Water/ICE tank — versatile, and with Ice Beam she is the textbook LANCE answer

hitmonlee.where: Saffron Fighting Dojo (pick one of two)
hitmonlee.note: a strong Fighting hitter — good vs the Normal/Rock/Ice types ahead

hitmonchan.where: Saffron Fighting Dojo (pick one of two)
hitmonchan.note: a strong Fighting hitter with elemental punches

snorlax.where: asleep on Routes 12 and 16 (only two in the game — Poke Flute to wake)
snorlax.note: a colossal Normal wall — enormous HP, learns almost anything, only two exist

magikarp.note: pathetic now, but becomes a GYARADOS — a Water/Flying beast worth the babysitting

aerodactyl.where: revive the Old Amber (Pewter Museum) on Cinnabar
aerodactyl.note: a rare fossil — blisteringly fast Rock/Flying attacker

omanyte.note: a fossil Rock/Water — tanky special attacker if you pick the Helix

kabuto.note: a fossil Rock/Water — Kabutops is a fast physical sweeper if you pick the Dome

clefairy.note: a rare Mt. Moon Normal-type with a huge, flexible movepool

vulpix.note: a solid Fire option if you skipped Growlithe

key_moves._doc: the handful of TMs/moves that change what the team can DO. The planner names these as a want when the matching threat is ahead and she has a good user for it.

ice beam.why: THE dragon-slayer — 4x on Lance's Dragonite and huge on his Dragonairs; also stellar neutral coverage. Teach it to Lapras or any Water-type

thunderbolt.why: hits Lorelei's water mons AND Lance's Gyarados/Aerodactyl hard; a clean Misty answer too

earthquake.where: Silph Co. (gift on 7F) / found in the Rocket Hideout
earthquake.why: the best Ground move in the game — smashes Surge, Blaine, Koga and Agatha's poison

psychic.where: Saffron gym reward (beat Sabrina) / Celadon Game Corner
psychic.why: nukes Koga's poison, Bruno's fighters and Agatha's ghosts

fire blast.why: a heavy Fire nuke — melts Erika and any Grass/Ice/Bug

surf.why: strong reliable Water STAB on top of opening the sea — great vs Brock, Blaine, Giovanni
